intro: Brassaia panayensis, also known as the Philippine Brassaia, is a traditional medicinal plant native to the Philippines. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Brassaia panayensis include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Brassaia panayensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the production of herbal tonics and syrups to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
